title: Charred Syrian Tomatoes Tagine
description: A flavorful and healthy charred tomato tagine featuring aromatic spices and a hint of sweetness. This dish is a perfect example of Syrian cuisine, with its rich and savory flavors.

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: Flaky sea salt for best flavor.

instructions: In a large Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until caramelized and dark brown, about 20 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute, until spices are fragrant.
Add the plum tomatoes, chopped, and cook, stirring occasionally, until they start to release their juices, about 5 minutes.
Transfer the Dutch oven to the preheated oven and roast, uncovered, for 25-30 minutes, until the tomatoes are charred and blistered.
Remove the Dutch oven from the oven and deglaze the fond with the water, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Stir in the chopped fresh parsley and lemon juice, and serve hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
Serve over couscous or rice, if desired.
